pois.name: Herkules-Denkmal
pois.description: The monumental copper statue of Hercules overlooks the Bergpark Wilhelmshöhe in Kassel. Constructed in the early 18th century, it serves as the crowning point of a massive water feature system.

pois.name: Kloster Lorsch
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its Carolingian gatehouse, the Torhalle. It is one of the most important architectural remnants of the post-Roman era in Germany.

pois.name: Mathildenhöhe Darmstadt
pois.description: An essential site for Art Nouveau, established as an artists' colony in 1899. It features the iconic Wedding Tower and the Russian Chapel.

pois.name: Frankfurter Römerberg
pois.description: The historic heart of Frankfurt, featuring the medieval Römer city hall and timber-framed houses. It has served as the site of imperial coronations for centuries.

pois.name: Limburger Dom
pois.description: A masterpiece of the Rhenish Transition style, combining Romanesque and early Gothic elements. Its seven towers dominate the skyline above the Lahn river.

pois.name: Kloster Eberbach
pois.description: A former Cistercian monastery in the Rheingau, famous for its Romanesque and Gothic architecture. It is widely recognized as one of the best-preserved medieval monastic complexes in Europe.

pois.name: Römerkastell Saalburg
pois.description: A reconstructed Roman fort on the Limes Germanicus, serving as a museum and research institute. It illustrates the daily life and military organization of the Roman frontier.

pois.name: Landgrafenschloss Marburg
pois.description: Perched high above the university town, this castle was the first residence of the Landgraviate of Hesse. It contains a significant Great Hall and a chapel with medieval frescoes.

pois.name: Fuldaer Dom
pois.description: The burial place of Saint Boniface and the former abbey church of Fulda Monastery. It was rebuilt in the Baroque style in the 18th century by Johann Dientzenhofer.

pois.name: Schloss Braunfels
pois.description: An iconic hilltop castle with a distinctive silhouette of towers and turrets. It has been the seat of the Counts of Solms-Braunfels for over 800 years.

pois.name: Sababurg
pois.description: Located in the Reinhardswald, this castle is famously associated with the fairy tale of Sleeping Beauty. It was originally built to protect pilgrims traveling to Gottsbüren.

pois.name: Keltenwelt am Glauberg
pois.description: An internationally significant Celtic archaeological site featuring a burial mound and a unique life-sized sandstone statue of a Celtic prince.

pois.name: Edersee Staumauer
pois.description: A massive masonry dam built to regulate the Weser river and provide electricity. It is a major engineering landmark of the early 20th century.

pois.name: Schloss Fasanerie
pois.description: Often cited as Hesse's most beautiful Baroque palace, located near Fulda. It houses a premier collection of antique ceramics and sculptures.

pois.name: Altstadt Büdingen
pois.description: One of Europe's best-preserved medieval town fortifications and old town centers. The town wall remains almost entirely intact with several towers and gates.

pois.name: Fritzlarer Dom
pois.description: St. Peter's Cathedral in Fritzlar stands where Boniface felled Donar's Oak. The current building represents a significant transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Schloss Weilburg
pois.description: A prominent Renaissance palace complex situated on a spur above the Lahn river. It features a notable orangery and terraced gardens.

pois.name: Idsteiner Altstadt
pois.description: Famous for its wealth of timber-framed houses, particularly the Killingerhaus. The 'Hexenturm' tower is the town's oldest remaining structure.

pois.name: Schloss Bad Homburg
pois.description: The former summer residence of the German Emperors, featuring a distinctive white tower from the medieval period. The palace reflects various styles from Baroque to Classicism.

pois.name: Freilichtmuseum Hessenpark
pois.description: An open-air museum showcasing over 100 relocated historic buildings from across Hesse. It preserves regional rural culture and traditional craftsmanship.

pois.name: Altes Rathaus Michelstadt
pois.description: This 1484 timber-framed town hall is an icon of German secular architecture. It is built on oak pillars, allowing the marketplace to function underneath.

pois.name: Felsenmeer Lautertal
pois.description: A vast field of blocky quartz-diorite boulders in the Odenwald. It was used by the Romans as a quarry for monumental columns.

pois.name: Niederwalddenkmal
pois.description: A massive monument overlooking the Rhine valley near Rüdesheim. It was built to commemorate the unification of Germany in 1871.

pois.name: Schloss Biebrich
pois.description: The Baroque residence of the Dukes of Nassau, located directly on the banks of the Rhine in Wiesbaden. Its grand park is a major local landmark.

pois.name: Burg Frankenstein
pois.description: A hilltop castle ruin associated with many local legends. It is often cited as an inspiration for Mary Shelley's famous novel, though the link remains debated.

pois.name: Heppenheimer Altstadt
pois.description: Known for its picturesque market square and the 'Cathedral of the Bergstraße'. The town features exceptionally well-preserved timber-framed architecture.

pois.name: Marienkirche Gelnhausen
pois.description: The main church of the former imperial city, showcasing the transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture. It contains significant medieval choir stalls.

pois.name: Burg Ronneburg
pois.description: A well-preserved hilltop fortress that was never destroyed. It offers an authentic look at late medieval and Renaissance fortification and living quarters.

pois.name: Kloster Arnsburg
pois.description: The atmospheric ruins of a Cistercian abbey near Lich. The roofless high Gothic church remains an impressive example of monastic architecture.

pois.name: Grube Messel
pois.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site and former oil shale quarry. It is famous for its exceptionally well-preserved fossils from the Eocene Epoch.

pois.name: Wasserkuppe
pois.description: The highest peak in Hesse and the Rhön Mountains. It is known as the 'birthplace of gliding' and offers panoramic views of the central German highlands.

pois.name: Point Alpha
pois.description: A former Cold War observation post on the border between Hesse and Thuringia. It preserves original fortifications and documents the history of the Iron Curtain.

pois.name: Herborner Altstadt
pois.description: Often called the 'Nassau Rothenburg', this town is famous for its colorful half-timbered houses and historic town gates.

pois.name: Burg Kronberg
pois.description: A medieval castle in the Taunus mountains with a rare double-chapel. It served as the ancestral seat of the powerful Knights of Kronberg.

pois.name: Schloss Philippsruhe
pois.description: A Baroque palace in Hanau situated on the Main river. It houses a museum dedicated to the Brothers Grimm and regional history.

pois.name: Wasserfestung Ziegenhain
pois.description: A unique example of a pentagonal water fortress. It served as a central military installation for the Landgraviate of Hesse-Kassel.

pois.name: Eschweger Marktplatz
pois.description: Known for its concentration of over 1,000 half-timbered houses. The marketplace is the cultural center of the town, featuring several historic administrative buildings.

pois.name: Bad Karlshafen Hafen
pois.description: A planned Baroque town founded by Landgrave Karl to house Huguenot refugees. Its central harbor basin is a unique feature of the local urban planning.

pois.name: Burg Herzberg
pois.description: Hesse's largest hilltop fortress, located in the Knüllgebirge. It features impressive bastions and towers from the transition between the Middle Ages and the Renaissance.

pois.name: Burgruine Friedewald
pois.description: A significant four-towered castle ruin that served as a hunting lodge for the Landgraves. Its architecture represents a transition to a more representative fortress.

pois.name: Auerbacher Schloss
pois.description: One of the most imposing castle ruins in the Bergstraße region. It is known for its high towers and a pine tree growing directly out of one of the castle walls.

pois.name: Burg Breuberg
pois.description: A massive fortress complex in the Odenwald, showcasing fortification styles from the Romanesque period to the Renaissance. It is exceptionally well-preserved.

pois.name: Einhard-Basilika Seligenstadt
pois.description: An important Carolingian church building commissioned by Einhard, the biographer of Charlemagne. It has been significantly preserved despite later additions.

pois.name: Isenburger Schloss
pois.description: A Renaissance palace in Offenbach am Main with a distinctive gallery facade. It is considered one of the most important secular Renaissance buildings north of the Alps.

pois.name: Burg Friedberg
pois.description: One of the largest castle complexes in Germany, occupying almost 4 hectares. It was an imperial castle (Reichsburg) and houses a significant Adolfsturm tower.

pois.name: Liebig-Museum
pois.description: The historic laboratory of chemist Justus von Liebig in Gießen. It remains as it was in the 19th century, serving as a landmark in the history of science.

pois.name: Alte Lahnbrücke Wetzlar
pois.description: A medieval stone bridge across the Lahn river, built in the 13th century. It is a key landmark of the historic old town of Wetzlar.

pois.name: Burg Runkel
pois.description: A dramatic castle ruin and inhabited palace overlooking the Lahn. It stands directly opposite the Schadeck Castle on the other side of the river.

pois.name: Rathaus Frankenberg
pois.description: A unique ten-towered timber-framed town hall, located in the center of Frankenberg's old town. It is a masterpiece of late Gothic craftsmanship.

pois.name: Rathaus Alsfeld
pois.description: A world-famous example of half-timbered architecture. This early 16th-century town hall stands on stone arches and is the emblem of the town.

pois.name: Kloster Haydau
pois.description: A former Cistercian nunnery that was converted into a hunting lodge. It has been restored and serves as a cultural venue in the Fulda valley.

pois.name: Burg Trendelburg
pois.description: A medieval castle known for its high tower, often associated with the Rapunzel legend. It is part of the German Fairy Tale Route.

pois.name: Kurhaus Wiesbaden
pois.description: A magnificent neoclassical building serving as the social center of the spa city. It houses the famous Wiesbaden Casino and grand concert halls.

pois.name: Tannenburg
pois.description: A well-preserved Staufer-era hilltop castle in eastern Hesse. It is a prime example of high medieval residential architecture.

pois.name: Stiftsruine Bad Hersfeld
pois.description: The largest Romanesque church ruin in the world. It serves as a spectacular venue for the annual Bad Hersfeld Theatre Festival.

pois.name: Stadtschloss Fulda
pois.description: The former residence of the Prince-Abbots and Prince-Bishops of Fulda. This Baroque palace complex includes grand historical rooms and a formal garden.

pois.name: Schloss Lichtenberg
pois.description: The first Renaissance castle built in southern Hesse. It houses the Odenwald Museum and offers wide views over the Fischbachtal.

pois.name: Elisabethkirche Marburg
pois.description: One of the earliest purely Gothic churches in the German-speaking world. It was built over the tomb of Saint Elizabeth of Hungary.

pois.name: Kaskaden im Bergpark
pois.description: The world-famous Baroque water cascades of the Bergpark Wilhelmshöhe. Water flows down a distance of 250 meters over stone steps to the castle.

pois.name: Brauhaus Knallhütte
pois.description: A historic brewery and inn where the Brothers Grimm collected fairy tales from storyteller Dorothea Viehmann. It has brewed beer since 1752.
